topics.name: 100 - 200 Level: Foundation Science, Anatomy, and Fundamentals of Nursing
topics.description: Anatomy, Physiology, Biochemistry, Microbiology, and Foundations of Professional Nursing.

subtopics.name: NUR 201: Foundations of Nursing and Health Assessment
objectives: Understand nursing history, nursing process (Assessment, Diagnosis, Planning, Implementation, Evaluation)
Perform vital signs measurement, physical health assessment, and aseptic technique infection control

subtopics.name: ANAT 201 & PHYS 201: Human Anatomy and Physiology for Nurses
objectives: Study structural anatomy and physiological mechanisms of human organ systems

topics.name: 300 Level: Medical-Surgical Nursing and Pharmacology
topics.description: Medical-Surgical Nursing I & II, Pharmacology, Pathology, and Clinical Practicum.

subtopics.name: NUR 301: Medical-Surgical Nursing
objectives: Manage nursing care for cardiovascular, respiratory, endocrine, renal, and gastrointestinal diseases
Perform peri-operative nursing care, wound management, and emergency clinical procedures

subtopics.name: PHARM 301: Pharmacology in Nursing Practice
objectives: Calculate drug dosages, administer medications via oral, IV, IM routes safely, and monitor side effects

topics.name: 400 - 500 Level: Maternal, Paediatric, Mental Health Nursing, and Final Board Defense
topics.description: Maternal & Child Health Nursing, Psychiatric Nursing, Community Health Nursing, and B.N.Sc. Thesis.

subtopics.name: NUR 401: Maternal & Child Health Nursing (Midwifery)
objectives: Provide comprehensive antenatal, intrapartum, postpartum nursing care, and newborn care

subtopics.name: NUR 501: Mental Health, Community Nursing, and Research Project
objectives: Manage psychiatric nursing interventions for mental health disorders
Execute community health assessment, epidemiology, disease surveillance, and defend original B.N.Sc. thesis
